pages.raw_text:
The council last night ordered
the board OF public works to enter
into immediate negotiations with
the Washington Water Power Co.
for a renewal of it's lighting con-
tract with the city. The old con-
tract expires Aug. 81.


Councilman N. s. Pratt, on whose
suggestion the matter was taken up,
favor a l year contract instead of
5 as before. Other councilmen who
favor the / year limit are Laumer,
Gray, Phillips and Baldwin.


During the past s years the w.
w. p. has charged "a apiece for arc
lights and agreed to keep each l
burning every night. In many In
stances they go out, but there Is
nothing in the old contract penaliz-
ing the company for this. The
lighting bill costs the city approx
mately 100000 per year. which Is
practically clear gain for the com
pany.


1t was suggested today that the
company will condescend to submit
two popositions-One for year at
IS per arc light and l for years at
the old rate. The board of public
works favors a 5-year contract with
the privilege Of cancellation on 81
